projects.label: Selected Work
projects.title: Case studies
projects.description: Real problems, engineered solutions, measurable results.
projects.analyticsProblem: Client needed real-time business metrics without slow legacy tools.
projects.analyticsSolution: Built a React dashboard with live WebSocket updates and custom charts.
projects.bookingProblem: Manual scheduling caused double bookings and lost revenue.
projects.bookingSolution: Developed a multi-tenant booking system with payments and notifications.
projects.bookingResult: Zero double bookings, 60% reduction in admin time.
projects.ecommerceProblem: Monolithic backend could not scale during peak traffic.
projects.ecommerceSolution: Designed async FastAPI microservice with caching and queue workers.

footer.tagline: Full-stack development team building fast, scalable and modern web applications.

faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: Quick answers to common questions about working together.
faq.q1: What technologies do you work with?
faq.a1: We specialize in modern full-stack development with React, TypeScript, and Python (FastAPI). Our stack includes PostgreSQL for data, C for systems programming, and modern DevOps practices with Docker. We choose tools based on project needs.
faq.q2: How long does a typical project take?
faq.a2: Timelines depend on scope. A landing page takes 1-2 weeks, a web application 3-6 weeks, and a full SaaS platform 6-12 weeks. We'll provide a detailed timeline after our initial consultation.
faq.q3: Do you provide support after launch?
faq.a3: Yes. Every project includes 30 days of post-launch support for bug fixes and minor adjustments. Extended maintenance and feature updates are available through a separate agreement.
faq.q4: How do we start working together?
faq.a4: It starts with a conversation. Reach out via the contact form or Telegram, tell us about your project, and we'll respond within 2 hours with a clear plan, timeline, and quote. No commitment needed.
faq.a5: We offer both fixed-price and hourly rates. Fixed-price works best for well-defined projects with clear requirements. Hourly is better for ongoing work, maintenance, or evolving projects. Free initial consultation included.
